violations.description: Collection-scoped Dapr fixture exposes shared fake publisher, dead-letter publisher, domain invoker, and command status store with no fixture-level reset hook.
violations.suggestion: Add ResetCollectedState() to the fixture and Reset/Clear methods to each fake; call before each DaprTestContainer test class.

violations.description: Static Lazy<Task<IConnectionMultiplexer>> persists Redis connection state outside the fixture lifecycle.
violations.suggestion: Move the multiplexer into a disposable fixture and clear only EventStore test keys during setup.

violations.description: Fixture mutates environment variables and restores them in DisposeAsync, but InitializeAsync has no catch/restore path if build/start fails.
violations.suggestion: Adopt the AspirePubSubProofTestFixture SnapshotAndSet/RestoreEnvironmentSnapshot try/catch pattern.

violations.description: Fault fixture mutates EnableKeycloak, environment, and sample fault flags before async startup completes.
violations.suggestion: Wrap startup in try/catch and restore the captured environment snapshot before rethrow.

violations.description: Keycloak fixture has the same env-var leak shape if InitializeAsync throws before DisposeAsync.
violations.suggestion: Use a shared TestEnvScope helper from Hexalith.EventStore.Testing.

violations.description: Constructor clears EVENTSTORE_ADMIN_* variables without snapshotting prior values.
violations.suggestion: Mirror GlobalOptionsBindingProfileTests and restore original values in Dispose.

recommendations: Centralize environment mutation behind a TestEnvScope helper with try/finally semantics.
Reset collection-scoped fake state before each test class.
Move static Redis connection ownership into a disposable fixture.
summary: Fixture investment is strong, but collection-scoped mutable state and environment mutation still create cross-test contamination risk.
